title: btc-graph content source audit
objective: Audit Bitcoin educational sources and expand btc-graph with atomic concepts and strict prerequisite edges.
notes: Canonical source of truth for the content discovery loop. Update this JSON first, then regenerate the workbook.

sources.name: Bitcoin Optech Topics
sources.scope: Topic index covering protocol, wallet, Lightning, fee, relay, and operational concepts.
sources.clusterStrategy: Track the index views plus individual topic pages for canonical naming, aliases, see-also links, and primary docs.
sources.notes: 157 topics plus 117 aliases make this a naming registry and gap detector more than a bulk node source. Implemented from this source in the current branch: protocol.x-only-public-keys, protocol.version-3-transaction-relay, and protocol.tapscript.

sources.name: BTC Transcripts Topics
sources.scope: Topic index into transcript-backed technical discussions and workshops.
sources.clusterStrategy: Track Topics, Categories, Types, and Sources as separate provenance surfaces before drilling into individual transcript families.
sources.notes: Use mainly for provenance and terminology normalization. Topic labels should not be imported 1:1.

sources.name: Bitcoin Dev Philosophy
sources.scope: Conceptual and systems-thinking source likely to surface bridge concepts and framing gaps.
sources.clusterStrategy: Track 9 chapter-level rows first, with later splits for dense chapters 5, 7, 8, and 9.
sources.notes: Heavy overlap with existing philosophy nodes, but strong for bridge concepts like validation sovereignty and consensus vs policy. Implemented from this source in the current branch: fundamentals.validation-sovereignty, protocol.consensus-vs-policy, and protocol.chain-split-risk.

sources.name: Mastering Bitcoin Book Map
sources.scope: Structured chapter map for foundational and implementation-oriented concept coverage.
sources.clusterStrategy: Track 14 chapter rows first, grouping chapters 4 to 12 as the primary extraction surface.
sources.notes: Already heavily reused in current nodes, so chapter titles are containers. Best current value is prerequisite ladders and bridge concepts. Implemented from this source in the current branch: protocol.light-client-trust-model and ops.full-node-vs-lightweight-wallet.

sources.name: Optech Schnorr/Taproot Workshop
sources.scope: Focused advanced curriculum around Schnorr, Taproot, and related prerequisite chains.
sources.clusterStrategy: Track the 12 module pages plus shared assets once per source.
sources.notes: Best current source for missing Taproot bridge concepts such as tagged hashes, taptweak, tapleaf inclusion proofs, and Huffman-optimized TapTrees. Implemented from this source in the current branch: protocol.tagged-hashes, protocol.taptweak, protocol.taptree, protocol.tapleaf-inclusion-proofs, and protocol.huffman-optimized-taptrees.

sources.name: Decoding by Bitcoin Devs
sources.scope: Structured educational material that may reveal curriculum-style prerequisite bundles.
sources.clusterStrategy: Track the Introduction, Tools, Transactions, Scripts, and Taproot module groups before lesson-level expansion.
sources.notes: Best used for sequencing and beginner-friendly prerequisite ordering rather than broad topic discovery. Implemented from this source in the current branch: protocol.p2pk, security.nonce-reuse-attack, protocol.p2pkh, protocol.op-checkmultisig, protocol.p2ms, and protocol.bare-multisig-standardness.

concepts.title: Tagged Hashes
concepts.atomicityRationale: A narrow cryptographic construction repeatedly used in Taproot and Schnorr material, distinct from generic hash functions.
concepts.prerequisiteRationale: Learners need the base hash-function mental model before namespace-separated tagged hashing makes sense.
concepts.notes: Implemented in the current branch as part of the first Taproot-internal bridge slice.

concepts.title: X-Only Public Keys
concepts.sourceContext: Topics index and Taproot-related topic pages
concepts.atomicityRationale: This is a specific key representation concept that bridges Schnorr math to Taproot output keys.
concepts.prerequisiteRationale: The concept depends on both general key knowledge and the Schnorr/Taproot context in which x-only keys matter.
concepts.notes: Implemented in the current branch as part of the first Taproot-internal bridge slice.

concepts.title: TapTweak
concepts.atomicityRationale: A concrete Taproot mechanism that is smaller than Taproot as a whole and useful for gap diagnosis.
concepts.prerequisiteRationale: The tweak mechanism is a Taproot-specific key derivation step that directly depends on Taproot context, tagged hashing, and x-only key encoding.
concepts.notes: Implemented in the current branch as part of the first Taproot-internal bridge slice.

concepts.title: TapTree
concepts.atomicityRationale: Specific Taproot script-tree structure, distinct from generic Merkle trees and from Taproot overall.
concepts.prerequisiteRationale: TapTree is best taught immediately after TapTweak. Merkle-tree knowledge remains useful context but is already reached transitively through the Taproot path, so the direct edge would be redundant.
concepts.notes: Implemented in the current branch as part of the first Taproot-internal bridge slice.

concepts.title: TapLeaf Inclusion Proofs
concepts.atomicityRationale: A concrete proof object within script-path spends, narrower than Taproot and narrower than TapTree.
concepts.prerequisiteRationale: The proof only makes sense after the learner understands the TapTree structure it proves against.
concepts.notes: Implemented in the current branch as part of the first Taproot-internal bridge slice.

concepts.title: Huffman-Optimized TapTrees
concepts.atomicityRationale: This is a narrow optimization concept that explains one specific design tradeoff inside Taproot trees.
concepts.prerequisiteRationale: The optimization is about arranging TapTree leaves, so the base tree concept is direct.
concepts.notes: Implemented in the current branch as the last explicit pending workshop optimization concept.

concepts.title: Chain Split Risk
concepts.sourceContext: Upgrading chapter 5.3.1 Costs of a split
concepts.atomicityRationale: This is one specific upgrade-risk concept about the costs of incompatible rule sets continuing in parallel, not a broad deployment-mechanisms umbrella.
concepts.prerequisiteRationale: Learners need the compatibility distinction between soft forks and hard forks before the consequences of a persistent split make sense.
concepts.notes: Implemented in the current branch as a narrow upgrade-risk bridge between protocol.softfork-hardfork and the existing activation-history case studies.

concepts.title: Tapscript
concepts.sourceContext: Topics alias normalization around Tapscript and Taproot script-path rules
concepts.atomicityRationale: This is the specific Taproot-era script ruleset, narrower than Taproot and cleaner than a broad script-path-spends explainer.
concepts.prerequisiteRationale: The direct learner need is the Taproot spend context. The base Script model is already inherited transitively through the SegWit and transaction-lifecycle path, so adding protocol.script directly would be redundant in the current graph.
concepts.notes: Implemented in the current branch as the next additive Taproot bridge without rewiring older phase-locked descendants such as protocol.op-checksigadd.

concepts.title: Version 3 Transaction Relay
concepts.sourceContext: Topics alias normalization around TRUC and version 3 relay
concepts.atomicityRationale: This is a distinct relay-policy concept that should not be collapsed into the broader package relay bucket.
concepts.prerequisiteRationale: The core learner need is to understand the pinning problem that v3 relay is trying to constrain. In the current graph, the policy boundary is already inherited transitively through the transaction-pinning branch, so adding it directly would be redundant.
concepts.notes: Implemented in the current branch as the next policy-leaf slice after adding protocol.consensus-vs-policy. Alias normalization remains mandatory here because Optech names and implementation jargon vary, and the direct policy-boundary edge was intentionally dropped after the redundancy check proved it was already inherited.

concepts.title: Validation Sovereignty
concepts.sourceContext: Trustlessness chapter and dont trust verify framing
concepts.atomicityRationale: A precise learner outcome about why running or relying on validation matters, not just a broad philosophy label.
concepts.prerequisiteRationale: It is a narrower applied consequence of trustlessness and verification culture.
concepts.notes: Implemented in the current branch as the philosophy-side bridge between trustlessness and practical self-validation.

concepts.title: Consensus vs Policy
concepts.sourceContext: Upgrade and split-risk discussion, reinforced by policy-heavy material in Mastering Bitcoin
concepts.atomicityRationale: A precise distinction that explains multiple later topics like relay, fee bumping, and upgrades.
concepts.prerequisiteRationale: Learners need the base idea of consensus rules before contrasting them with local relay policy.
concepts.notes: Implemented in the current branch after the Taproot-internal slice. This is the cleanest conceptual parent for the existing policy and relay cluster.

concepts.title: Address Reuse Harms
concepts.sourceContext: Wallet, privacy, and address-format clusters; reinforced by philosophy privacy chapter
concepts.atomicityRationale: Narrow user-facing privacy consequence that is more actionable than a broad privacy umbrella.
concepts.prerequisiteRationale: The harm is best understood once the learner knows what addresses are and why pseudonymity is fragile.
concepts.notes: Do not add as a separate node. Fold any improved copy or resources into privacy.address-reuse.

concepts.title: Timelock Taxonomy
concepts.sourceContext: Authorization and transaction chapters covering CLTV, CSV, and sequence-based relative timelocks
concepts.atomicityRationale: A useful bridge concept that organizes multiple already-existing timelock mechanisms into one mental model.
concepts.prerequisiteRationale: The taxonomy should hang off the existing general timelock node rather than duplicate specific mechanism nodes.
concepts.notes: Do not add as a separate node. Enrich protocol.timelocks to make the taxonomy more explicit.

concepts.title: Light Client Trust Model
concepts.sourceContext: Network and blockchain chapters covering SPV, compact block filters, and lightweight-client tradeoffs
concepts.atomicityRationale: This is a specific tradeoff concept that bridges full-node verification to lightweight client behavior.
concepts.prerequisiteRationale: The trust model only makes sense once the learner can contrast full-node guarantees with filtered-client shortcuts.
concepts.notes: Implemented in the current branch as a thin conceptual bridge without rewiring older phase-locked neighbor nodes.

concepts.title: Full Node vs Lightweight Wallet
concepts.sourceContext: Introduction chapter discussion of full-node versus lightweight operating modes
concepts.atomicityRationale: This is a concrete learner decision point with one clear outcome: understand how wallet mode changes verification guarantees and trust assumptions.
concepts.prerequisiteRationale: Learners need both the user-facing reason to self-verify and the mechanism-level trust tradeoffs before this comparison becomes meaningful.
concepts.notes: Implemented in the current branch as the first downstream consumer for the light-client trust branch.

concepts.title: Nonce Reuse Attack
concepts.sourceContext: Taproot module page Nonce Reuse Attack
concepts.atomicityRationale: Specific failure mode inside Schnorr signing, small enough to diagnose as its own knowledge gap.
concepts.prerequisiteRationale: The learner needs the signature flow before the nonce leakage failure is meaningful.
concepts.notes: Implemented in the current branch as the next Decoding-derived security slice.

concepts.title: Pay to Public Key (P2PK)
concepts.atomicityRationale: Specific historical script pattern that should not be hidden inside broader script umbrella nodes.
concepts.prerequisiteRationale: In the current graph, Bitcoin Script already brings the learner to the locking and unlocking model that makes a direct public-key output meaningful. Adding public-private-keys directly would duplicate that background transitively.
concepts.notes: Implemented in the current branch as the cleanest Decoding-derived script-history slice.

concepts.title: Pay to Multisig (P2MS)
concepts.atomicityRationale: Specific bare multisig pattern, narrower than generic multisig and distinct from P2SH-based multisig.
concepts.prerequisiteRationale: Bare multisig is the concrete script template built around OP_CHECKMULTISIG, so the opcode mechanism is the clean direct parent once it exists in the graph.
concepts.notes: Implemented in the current branch after adding protocol.op-checkmultisig as its direct protocol-side parent.

concepts.title: OP_CHECKMULTISIG
concepts.atomicityRationale: A specific opcode-level mechanism that is smaller than bare multisig as a whole and useful for diagnosing why legacy multisig scripts are structured the way they are.
concepts.prerequisiteRationale: The learner needs the base Script execution model before a multisignature verification opcode and its stack behavior make sense.
concepts.notes: Implemented in the current branch as the mechanism bridge that parents protocol.p2ms.

concepts.title: Pay to Public Key Hash (P2PKH)
concepts.atomicityRationale: Specific legacy output template that is narrower than broad address or script overviews and distinct from both P2PK and P2SH.
concepts.prerequisiteRationale: The learner benefits from first seeing the simpler direct-public-key output and then adding the hash-based locking step that differentiates P2PKH.
concepts.notes: Implemented in the current branch as the next Decoding-derived output-template slice.

concepts.title: Bare Multisig Standardness
concepts.sourceContext: Scripts module page P2MS, especially the standardness-versus-validity discussion
concepts.atomicityRationale: A narrow policy leaf about one specific script template's default relay treatment, distinct from both the bare multisig template itself and the broader concept of standardness policy.
concepts.prerequisiteRationale: Learners need the bare multisig template and the general idea of standardness before the special relay treatment of bare multisig becomes meaningful.
concepts.notes: Implemented in the current branch as the first policy leaf under the new legacy multisig mini-subgraph.

concepts.title: Cluster Mempool
concepts.atomicityRationale: The source confirms this is already a stable concept in the graph, so the work here is provenance and aliasing rather than a new node.
concepts.notes: Duplicate detected. Use the source as enrichment only.

concepts.title: Silent Payments
concepts.atomicityRationale: Already present locally, so the source should improve aliases, provenance, or resources rather than create a new node.
concepts.notes: Duplicate detected.

concepts.title: Package Relay
concepts.atomicityRationale: Already in the graph. The new work is to map related sources and separate it cleanly from v3 relay and cluster mempool.
concepts.notes: Duplicate detected.

concepts.title: Miniscript
concepts.atomicityRationale: Already represented. The source is useful for enrichment and related-source mapping.
concepts.notes: Duplicate detected.

concepts.title: BOLT12 Offers
concepts.atomicityRationale: Existing node confirmed. The source is valuable for terminology normalization from Offers to BOLT12 Offers.
concepts.notes: Duplicate detected.

edges.rationale: TapTweak is defined using tagged hashes directly in Taproot key tweaking.
edges.notes: Source-driven bridge edge.

edges.rationale: TapTweak operates on x-only public keys in the Taproot construction.
edges.notes: Source-driven bridge edge.

edges.rationale: The inclusion proof only makes sense after the learner understands the TapTree structure.
edges.notes: Source-driven bridge edge.

edges.rationale: Huffman optimization is a refinement of how TapTree leaves are arranged.
edges.notes: Source-driven bridge edge.

edges.rationale: Split risk is a downstream consequence of incompatible upgrade paths, so learners should understand the compatibility boundary first.
edges.notes: Direct edge chosen over activation-history case studies because those are downstream examples, not prerequisites.

edges.rationale: Tapscript is a Taproot-specific script version, so learners need the Taproot spend context before its rule changes make sense.
edges.notes: Direct edge keeps the node scoped to the Taproot family without collapsing it into a generic script refresher.

edges.rationale: Version 3 relay is a targeted policy response to transaction pinning, so learners should understand the attack class before the mitigation.
edges.notes: Direct edge chosen over package-relay because the pinning problem is the narrower immediate teaching need.

edges.rationale: Validation sovereignty is an applied consequence of trustlessness and verification culture.
edges.notes: Bridge edge inferred from philosophy sequencing.

edges.rationale: Learners need the full-node baseline before they can understand which validation guarantees a light client gives up.
edges.notes: Bridge edge inferred from full-node versus lightweight-client contrast.

edges.rationale: Compact block filters provide the modern filtered-client mechanism that anchors the trust-model comparison.
edges.notes: Kept additive and conceptual instead of rewiring older light-client mechanism leaves in the same slice.

edges.rationale: The comparison only matters once the learner understands why self-validation is valuable in the first place.
edges.notes: User-facing prerequisite edge for the wallet-mode decision.

edges.rationale: Learners need the mechanism-level trust tradeoffs before comparing full-node wallets with lightweight clients.
edges.notes: First downstream consumer for the light-client trust branch.

edges.rationale: P2PK is a concrete legacy script template, so learners first need the locking and unlocking model from Bitcoin Script.
edges.notes: Chosen as the first script-history leaf from Decoding after dedupe and direct-edge review.

edges.rationale: The nonce-reuse failure only makes sense after the learner understands the Schnorr signing flow that produces the nonce and signature pair.
edges.notes: Chosen as a narrow additive security slice without bundling it to custody-side defenses.

edges.rationale: P2PKH is easiest to understand as the hashed-public-key refinement of a direct public-key output.
edges.notes: Chosen to preserve the output-template ladder without repeating the broader Script prerequisite directly.

edges.rationale: Hash-based locking only makes sense once the learner understands what a hash commits to and why it can stand in for the public key until spend time.
edges.notes: Required to keep the node about public-key hashing rather than just script syntax.

edges.rationale: OP_CHECKMULTISIG is an opcode-level script mechanic, so learners first need the general Script model and stack execution basics.
edges.notes: Chosen as the mechanism bridge before protocol.p2ms.

edges.rationale: Bare multisig is the concrete locking template built from OP_CHECKMULTISIG, so the opcode is the clean direct parent once introduced.
edges.notes: Replaces the earlier curricular and wallet-semantic parent candidates.

edges.rationale: The relay treatment only makes sense once the learner understands what a bare multisig output actually is on-chain.
edges.notes: Policy leaf anchored on the specific legacy script template it constrains.

edges.rationale: This node is a concrete application of standard transaction policy, so the general standardness concept should come first.
edges.notes: Policy-boundary prerequisite inherited through the existing mempool-policy branch.

decisions.subject: source.bitcoinops-topics
decisions.rationale: Use Bitcoin Optech Topics as a canonical naming registry, alias map, related-topic graph, and update feed rather than as a bulk node importer.
decisions.notes: Heavy existing reuse means most value is in dedupe and alias normalization.

decisions.subject: taproot-internal-bridge-concepts
decisions.rationale: Prioritize missing Taproot bridge nodes from the workshop instead of broad Taproot duplicates.
decisions.notes: This source is narrower but pedagogically stronger than the general topics index.

decisions.subject: implemented-first-node-slice
decisions.rationale: Start implementation with the tightest coherent subgraph: Taproot internal bridge concepts that were repeatedly surfaced across Optech and Decoding and had the lowest duplicate risk.
decisions.notes: Implemented in the current branch before the broader policy-bridge slice.

decisions.subject: source.btctranscripts-topics
decisions.rationale: Use BTC Transcripts for provenance, source-family mapping, and terminology normalization before minting new nodes.
decisions.notes: The topic index mixes concept nodes with source-format artifacts and organizations.

decisions.subject: source.bitcoindevs-decoding
decisions.rationale: Use Decoding as a sequencing and prerequisite-ordering source first, and only mint nodes from narrow lesson pages that survive dedupe.
decisions.notes: The strongest value is clean educational boundaries and beginner-to-intermediate ordering.

decisions.subject: source.bitcoin-dev-philosophy
decisions.rationale: Track the source by chapter containers rather than turning chapter titles into nodes. Split dense chapters later only when needed.
decisions.notes: Broad philosophy headers are too umbrella-like for direct ingestion.

decisions.subject: source.mastering-bitcoin-book-map
decisions.rationale: Track Mastering Bitcoin by chapter first and treat chapters as extraction containers, not node candidates.
decisions.notes: The book already has heavy overlap in current resources, so the main value is bridge concepts and prerequisite ladders.

decisions.subject: source-alias-normalization
decisions.rationale: Normalize aliases before adding nodes so jargon variants such as TRUC versus version 3 transaction relay and Offers versus BOLT12 Offers do not create duplicates.
decisions.notes: This is a core dedupe rule for the loop.

decisions.subject: protocol.consensus-vs-policy
decisions.rationale: The next bridge node after the Taproot-internal slice should be protocol.consensus-vs-policy because it gives the existing mempool and relay branch a missing conceptual parent without duplicating existing leaves.
decisions.notes: Implemented in the current branch. The immediate follow-up rewires target ops.mempool-policy, protocol.rbf, protocol.dust-policy, and protocol.cpfp-carve-out.

decisions.subject: privacy.address-reuse-harms
decisions.rationale: The existing privacy.address-reuse node already occupies the exact bridge position this candidate wants and already teaches the harm model.
decisions.notes: Future work should refine the existing node instead of minting a duplicate.

decisions.subject: protocol.timelock-taxonomy
decisions.rationale: protocol.timelocks already acts as the central bridge node for the absolute versus relative timelock family, so a second taxonomy wrapper would be redundant.
decisions.notes: Future work should enrich protocol.timelocks copy and resources instead of adding a second node.

decisions.subject: policy-subtree-parent-edges
decisions.rationale: The first policy-bridge rewire should target the nodes where the consensus-versus-policy boundary is most direct: ops.mempool-policy, protocol.rbf, protocol.dust-policy, and protocol.cpfp-carve-out. Deeper leaves should inherit the bridge transitively for now.
decisions.notes: This keeps the subtree clearer without over-wiring every downstream policy leaf at once.

decisions.subject: per-node-amazon-book-resource
decisions.rationale: Each node should expose at least one Amazon-linked book resource so learners have a long-form study option in addition to articles. When a node was already at its resource cap, the weakest non-book resource was replaced instead of bloating the list.
decisions.notes: The pass used only book links already established elsewhere in the repo, normalized chapter-link book entries to title-level Amazon links, and added a test to keep future nodes covered.

decisions.subject: protocol.version-3-transaction-relay
decisions.rationale: Version 3 transaction relay is the highest-confidence remaining concept from the audited seed sources because it sharpens the existing mempool policy branch without opening a new broad subtree. In the current graph it fits best as a direct child of transaction pinning, with the policy boundary already inherited transitively.
decisions.notes: Kept this slice additive for now instead of rewiring deeper descendants like protocol.ephemeral-anchors until the relay-policy branch grows further.

decisions.subject: validation-and-light-client-bridges
decisions.rationale: The next thin branch after the relay-policy slice is the trust and verification path. fundamentals.validation-sovereignty is the cleanest philosophy-side dependent to add now, and protocol.light-client-trust-model can be introduced as a narrow conceptual bridge without forcing rewires onto older phase-locked light-client nodes.
decisions.notes: Kept this slice additive after subagent review showed that rewiring legacy neighbors like protocol.bloom-filters-bip37 would add overlap without enough downstream surface yet.

decisions.subject: ops.full-node-vs-lightweight-wallet
decisions.rationale: The cleanest next consumer for protocol.light-client-trust-model is a user-facing comparison between full nodes and lightweight wallets. It strengthens the thin trust branch without forcing rewires on older phase-locked mechanism nodes.
decisions.notes: Chosen over further light-client neighbor rewires because phase-5, phase-6, and phase-11 constraints make those edits more brittle than adding a new downstream consumer.

decisions.subject: protocol.chain-split-risk
decisions.rationale: The cleanest next philosophy-derived additive slice is protocol.chain-split-risk. It isolates one specific upgrade-risk outcome from the broader deployment-mechanisms discussion and creates a bridge from protocol.softfork-hardfork to the existing activation-history case studies without duplicating them.
decisions.notes: Chosen over a broader future-deployment-mechanisms wrapper because the split-cost concept is more atomic and easier to place cleanly in the current graph.

decisions.subject: protocol.tapscript
decisions.rationale: The cleanest next Taproot-derived additive slice is protocol.tapscript. It captures the specific BIP342 script ruleset without duplicating generic Taproot overview material or forcing rewires onto older phase-locked descendants such as protocol.op-checksigadd.
decisions.notes: Chosen over a synthetic taproot-script-path-spends wrapper because Tapscript is a canonical source-native concept with cleaner boundaries in the current graph. The direct protocol.script edge was dropped after the redundancy check showed it was already inherited transitively through the Taproot branch.

decisions.subject: protocol.p2pk
decisions.rationale: The cleanest next Decoding-derived additive slice is protocol.p2pk. It fills a specific historical script-template gap without forcing direct-edge violations or overlapping too heavily with existing wallet-level multisig coverage.
decisions.notes: Deferred protocol.p2ms because its cleanest current prerequisite options are either wallet-semantic overlap or a curricular, non-direct dependency on protocol.p2pk.

decisions.subject: security.nonce-reuse-attack
decisions.rationale: The cleanest next Decoding-derived additive slice after P2PK is security.nonce-reuse-attack. It adds a precise Schnorr signing failure mode without reopening the brittle Taproot prerequisite chain or forcing a phase-11 custody rewire.
decisions.notes: Kept additive and separate from security.exfiltration-resistant-signing so the graph distinguishes the failure mode from the defense pattern.

decisions.subject: protocol.p2pkh
decisions.rationale: The next clean Decoding-derived output-template slice is protocol.p2pkh. It fills an important historical and conceptual gap between direct public-key locking and later script-hash patterns without collapsing into wallet UX or broad address-format overviews.
decisions.notes: This does not resolve the protocol-side bridge for protocol.p2ms by itself. The likely next comparison remains protocol.op-checkmultisig versus protocol.p2ms directly.

decisions.subject: protocol.op-checkmultisig
decisions.rationale: The cleanest next Decoding-derived mechanism slice is protocol.op-checkmultisig. It isolates the legacy multisig verification opcode, explains the OP_0 off-by-one quirk, and gives protocol.p2ms a direct protocol-side parent instead of relying on curricular sequencing or wallet semantics.
decisions.notes: Chosen before and alongside protocol.p2ms so the graph distinguishes the opcode mechanism from the bare multisig script template and from downstream standardness-policy facts.

decisions.subject: protocol.p2ms
decisions.rationale: After introducing protocol.op-checkmultisig, protocol.p2ms becomes a clean concrete template node with a protocol-side parent. This keeps the graph focused on on-chain bare multisig mechanics without pulling in wallet-semantic multisig or downstream policy leaves.
decisions.notes: Kept the direct parent to protocol.op-checkmultisig rather than protocol.script so the graph can distinguish the opcode mechanism from the template built on top of it.

decisions.subject: protocol.bare-multisig-standardness
decisions.rationale: The first downstream policy leaf after the legacy multisig mini-subgraph should be protocol.bare-multisig-standardness. It turns the Decoding standardness-versus-validity sidebar into a precise graph node without collapsing it into generic policy or wallet-level multisig content.
decisions.notes: Kept protocol.consensus-vs-policy out of the direct prerequisites because that boundary is already inherited through protocol.standardness-policy.
